revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '北秋津横穴墓群は８基。 内１基は終戦の直前に内野弘氏によって発掘されました。'
revision.text: 北秋津横穴墓群は８基。 内１基は終戦の直前に内野弘氏によって発掘されました。
北秋津横穴墓群は８基。 内１基は終戦の直前に内野弘氏によって発掘されました。 北秋津横穴墓群の残りの７基はまだ発掘中だそうです。
{{SD|定義になっていない}} 北秋津横穴墓群は８基。 内１基は終戦の直前に内野弘氏によって発掘されました。 北秋津横穴墓群の残りの７基はまだ発掘中だそうです。
{{SD|定義になっていない}} '''北秋津横穴墓群'''（きたあきつよこあなぼぐん、きたあきつおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]][[北秋津]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。 北秋津横穴墓群は8基。 内1基は[[終戦の日|終戦]]の直前に[[内野弘]]によって[[発掘調査|発掘]]された。 北秋津横穴墓群の残りの7基はまだ発掘中。

revision.comment: SDは微妙なので、具体的な問題点を指摘。必要であれば[[WP:AFD]]等へ。
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2009-8 | 特筆性 = 2009-8 | 加筆 = 2009-8 }}
'''北秋津横穴墓群'''（きたあきつよこあなぼぐん、きたあきつおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]][[北秋津]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。 北秋津横穴墓群は8基。 内1基は[[終戦の日|終戦]]の直前に[[内野弘]]によって[[発掘調査|発掘]]された。 北秋津横穴墓群の残りの7基はまだ発掘中。

{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2009-8 | 特筆性 = 2009-8 | 加筆 = 2009-8 }}
'''北秋津横穴墓群'''（きたあきつよこあなぼぐん、きたあきつおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]][[北秋津]]にある[[古墳時代#古墳時代後期|古墳時代後期]]の[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
当時の埋葬施設のあり方を研究する上で貴重な歴史的価値を持つものとされており、[[1976年]]（[[昭和]]51年）に埼玉県の[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|重要遺跡]]に選定された。この古墳を含む一帯は[[土地区画整理事業]]の予定地になっているが、埼玉県は、所沢市が提出した所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書に対して「『北秋津横穴墓群』については開発を行わず、現状保存に努めること」との意見を付している。
北秋津横穴墓群は8基。 内1基は[[終戦の日|終戦]]の直前に[[内野弘]]によって[[発掘調査|発掘]]された。 北秋津横穴墓群の残りの7基はまだ発掘中。

{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2009-8 | 特筆性 = 2009-8 | 加筆 = 2009-8 }}
'''北秋津横穴墓群'''（きたあきつよこあなぼぐん、きたあきつおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]][[北秋津]]から[[上安松]]にかけて存在する[[古墳時代#古墳時代後期|古墳時代後期]]の[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
== 概要 == [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[5月3日]]、[[陸軍所沢飛行場]]の物資を疎開させるための地下壕造成の際に1基が発見され<ref>斎藤脩治 『埼玉ふるさと散歩<所沢市>』 さきたま出版会<さきたま双書>、1993年、31頁。</ref>、[[所沢市立所沢小学校|所沢国民学校]]に勤務していた[[内野弘]]によって[[発掘調査]]が行われた<ref name="市史">所沢市史編さん委員会編 『所沢市史 上』 所沢市、1991年、217-219頁。</ref>。
この横穴墓は、斜面の中腹に開けられた縦横約0.7メートル、奥行約1.5メートルの羨道の先に、長径2メートル短径1.5メートルの楕円形で高さ約0.9メートルの玄室が配置されている。羨道と玄室の間は[[柳瀬川]]の川原石で封鎖され、床に川原石が敷き詰められた玄室には、頭を北に向けた身長約1.65メートルの男性が伸葬されていた。遺体の右側にも追葬されたとみられる大小2個の頭蓋骨があったが、[[副葬品]]はみつかっていない。<ref>前掲 斎藤(1993)、33頁。</ref>
この1基の他に[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の所在調査によって新たに7基の存在が確認されている<ref name="市史"/>。
== 評価 == 当時の埋葬施設のあり方を研究する上で貴重な歴史的価値を持つものとされており<ref>「所沢市議会議事録 平成17年3月定例会」（3月14日、所沢市教育長による答弁）[http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>、[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に埼玉県の[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|重要遺跡]]に選定された。
この古墳を含む一帯は[[土地区画整理事業]]の予定地になっているが、埼玉県は、所沢市が提出した「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書」に対して「『北秋津横穴墓群』については開発を行わず、現状保存に努めること」との意見を付している<ref>埼玉県平成16年環推第35号（平成16年4月20日付） 「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書について」[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|埼玉県選定重要遺跡一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:きたあきつよこあなほくん}} [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の歴史]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:所沢市]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{japan-geo-stub}}

revision.comment: 問題点は改善されたと思います

この古墳を含む一帯は[[土地区画整理事業]]の予定地になっているが、埼玉県は、所沢市が提出した「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書」に対して「『北秋津横穴墓群』については開発を行わず、現状保存に努めること」との意見を付している<ref>埼玉県平成16年環推第35号（平成16年4月20日付） 「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書について」[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>。

この1基の他に[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の所在調査によって新たに7基の存在が確認されている<ref name="市史"/>。
== 評価 == 当時の埋葬施設のあり方を研究する上で貴重な歴史的価値を持つものとされており<ref>「所沢市議会議事録 平成17年3月定例会」（3月14日、所沢市教育長による答弁）[http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>、[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に埼玉県の[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|重要遺跡]]に選定された。
この古墳を含む一帯は[[土地区画整理事業]]の予定地になっているが、埼玉県は、所沢市が提出した「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書」に対して「『北秋津横穴墓群』については開発を行わず、現状保存に努めること」との意見を付している<ref>埼玉県平成16年環推第35号（平成16年4月20日付） 「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書について」[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|埼玉県選定重要遺跡一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:きたあきつよこあなほくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{japan-geo-stub}}

'''北秋津横穴墓群'''（きたあきつよこあなぼぐん、きたあきつおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]][[北秋津]]から[[上安松]]にかけて存在する[[古墳時代#古墳時代後期|古墳時代後期]]の[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
== 概要 == [[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[5月3日]]、[[陸軍所沢飛行場]]の物資を疎開させるための地下壕造成の際に1基が発見され<ref>斎藤脩治 『埼玉ふるさと散歩<所沢市>』 さきたま出版会<さきたま双書>、1993年、31頁。</ref>、[[所沢市立所沢小学校|所沢国民学校]]に勤務していた[[内野弘]]によって[[発掘調査]]が行われた<ref name="市史">所沢市史編さん委員会編 『所沢市史 上』 所沢市、1991年、217-219頁。</ref>。
この横穴墓は、斜面の中腹に開けられた縦横約0.7メートル、奥行約1.5メートルの[[羨道]]の先に、長径2メートル、短径1.5メートルの楕円形で高さ約0.9メートルの玄室が配置されている。羨道と玄室の間は[[柳瀬川]]の川原石で封鎖され、床に川原石が敷き詰められた玄室には、頭を北に向けた身長約1.65メートルの男性が伸葬されていた。遺体の右側にも追葬されたとみられる大小2個の頭蓋骨があったが、[[副葬品]]はみつかっていない。<ref>前掲 斎藤(1993)、33頁。</ref>
この1基の他に[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の所在調査によって新たに7基の存在が確認されている<ref name="市史"/>。
== 評価 == 当時の埋葬施設のあり方を研究する上で貴重な歴史的価値を持つものとされており<ref>「所沢市議会議事録 平成17年3月定例会」（3月14日、所沢市教育長による答弁）[http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>、[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に埼玉県の[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|重要遺跡]]に選定された。
この古墳を含む一帯は[[土地区画整理事業]]の予定地になっているが、埼玉県は、所沢市が提出した「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書」に対して「『北秋津横穴墓群』については開発を行わず、現状保存に努めること」との意見を付している<ref>埼玉県平成16年環推第35号（平成16年4月20日付） 「所沢市北秋津地区土地区画整理事業に係る戦略的環境影響評価報告書について」[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|埼玉県選定重要遺跡一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
