[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]]
{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.

The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}

'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
