revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Fixing the kk issue]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
[[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Clustered burial mounds]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa City]]
[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Removing this excess]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
[[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Clustered burial mounds]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa City]]
[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 2]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
[[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Clustered Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa City]]
[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
[[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Clustered Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa City]]
[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.

'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Sendou|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
