<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|qid=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|qid=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|qid=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|qid=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|qid=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|qid=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|qid=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|qid=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

[[ja:横穴墓]]
<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.

Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

National Historic Site

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
