<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.

Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11403899]] Wikidata name: Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|Kashiya Cave Tombs|WD=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|en|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|WD=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site

== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|Kashiya Cave Tombs|WD=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|en|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|WD=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])

Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - '''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.

In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786|1=Kami-Yasumatsu}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897|1=Kita-Akitsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#県選定重要遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Archaeological Sites of Saitama Prefecture|1=Q11426571|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426571}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=list of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group|1=Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209840}} - A similar site located along the Yanase River.
{{Coord|35|46|58.6|N|139|29|2.7|E|region:JP|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high coama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoanabos|en|Kashiya Yokoanabos|WD=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Yokoanabos|1=Kashiya Yokoanabos}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786|1=Kami-Yasumatsu}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897|1=Kita-Akitsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|WD=Q11358786|1=Kami-Yasumatsu}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|WD=Q11403897|1=Kita-Akitsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&PWD=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

<!--Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster--><!--Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11403899}} [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kami-Yasumatsu|qid=Q11358786|1=Kami-Yasumatsu}}; {{ill|Kita-Akitsu|qid=Q11403897|1=Kita-Akitsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.782944|N|139.484083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (北秋津横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] from the [[Late Kofun period]] located between [[Kita-Akitsu, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kita-Akitsu]] and [[Kamiyamazato, Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture|Kamiyamazato]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== Overview == On [[May 3, 1945]] (Shōwa 20), one tomb was discovered during the excavation of an underground bunker for relocating supplies at the [[Imperial Japanese Army Tokorozawa Airfield]]<ref>Saito, Shuji. 'Saitama Furusato Sanpo: Tokorozawa City'. Sakitama Publishing, 1993, p.31.</ref>. The tomb was investigated by [[Hiroshi Uchino]], who was working at [[Tokorozawa National School]] at the time<ref name="Tokorozawa City History">Tokorozawa City History Editorial Committee, 'Tokorozawa City History Volume 1', Tokorozawa City, 1991, pp. 217-219.</ref>.
This tomb consists of a [[Endō|dromos]] (羨道) approximately 0.7 meters in width and height and 1.5 meters in depth, leading to an elliptical burial chamber (玄室) measuring 2 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and about 0.9 meters in height. The entrance between the dromos and the burial chamber was sealed with river stones from the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], and the chamber floor was covered with river stones. Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&Pqid=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.

Inside, a man approximately 1.65 meters tall was found buried in an extended supine position with his head facing north. Two additional skulls of different sizes, believed to be secondary burials, were also discovered, but no grave goods were found<ref>Saito (1993), p.33.</ref>.
In addition to this tomb, a 1985 (Shōwa 60) site survey confirmed the existence of seven more tombs in the area<ref name="Tokorozawa City History"/>.
== Significance == The site holds valuable historical significance for studying burial practices of the time<ref>"Tokorozawa City Council Meeting Minutes, March 2005 Regular Session" (March 14, 2005, response by Tokorozawa City Board of Education) [http://thd.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/kaigiroku/cgi-bin/WWWframeNittei.exe?A=frameNittei&USR=webusr&Pqid=&XM=000000000000000&L=1&S=15&Y=%95%bd%90%ac17%94%4e&B=-1&T=-1&T0=-1&O=-1&P1=&P2=&P3=&P=1&K=26&N=217&W1=&W2=&W3=&W4=&DU=0&WDT=0]</ref>. In {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was designated as an [[Important Archaeological Site of Saitama Prefecture]] under the [[List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefectural Cultural Properties List]].
Although the site is part of a planned [[land readjustment project]], Saitama Prefecture has advised Tokorozawa City to preserve the "Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group" in its original state and avoid development, as noted in the "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" submitted by Tokorozawa City<ref>Saitama Prefecture Document No. 35, April 20, 2004, "Strategic Environmental Impact Assessment Report on the Tokorozawa City Kita-Akitsu District Land Readjustment Project" [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/A09/BA00/asesu/2_jirei/S002/S002_iken2.pdf]</ref>.
